stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The Aral Sea, located in Central Asia, was once one of the largest inland bodies of water on Earth.
  • Ancient travelers described it as a vast lake stretching across the horizon.
  • Today, the Aral Sea covers only a fraction of its former area.
  • Beginning in the 1960s, government planners diverted the rivers that fed the sea in order to irrigate large cotton fields, causing the sea to shrink dramatically.
stem: The student wants to specify the reason the Aral Sea’s area changed. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Ancient travelers described the Aral Sea, which sits in Central Asia, as a vast lake stretching across the horizon.
Beginning in the 1960s, government planners diverted the rivers feeding the Aral Sea to irrigate cotton fields, causing the sea to shrink dramatically.
Although it was once one of the largest inland bodies of water on Earth, the Aral Sea now covers only a fraction of its former area.
The Aral Sea is located in Central Asia, but it now covers far less area than ancient travelers once observed.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence specifies the reason the Aral Sea’s area changed, noting that beginning in the 1960s government planners diverted the rivers feeding the sea to irrigate cotton fields, which caused it to shrink dramatically.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes how ancient travelers characterized the Aral Sea; it doesn’t specify the reason the sea’s area changed. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence contrasts the sea’s past and present extent; it doesn’t specify the reason the area changed. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence notes the sea’s location and that it has shrunk, but it doesn’t identify the cause of the change.
